description: Hot-reload cron body at fire time by re-reading filePath via parseCronFile, with cached-body fallback on error, so crons/*.md edits apply without a runtime restart. Harness-infra only (scripts/, crons/, tests, docs).

userStories.title: loadCrons stores a dir-qualified filePath
userStories.description: As the cron runtime, I need each CronEntry.filePath to be a path re-readable from any cwd, because today it is a bare basename that would break reloadBody.
acceptanceCriteria: loadCrons populates filePath with path.join(dir, f) (e.g. crons/heartbeat.md), not the bare filename f (currently parseCronFile receives f at line 54 and stores it verbatim as filePath at line 45)
Derived id is unchanged: parseCronFile derives id via path.basename(file, '.md'), identical for 'crons/heartbeat.md' and 'heartbeat.md'
Existing loadCrons tests still pass (suite uses loadCrons(tmp), so filePath becomes <tmp>/<name>.md); update only an assertion that checks the exact filePath value, if any exists
Typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Export reloadBody helper owning read + diff + log + fallback
userStories.description: As the cron runtime, I want one exported helper that returns the latest on-disk body, emits the reload/error log signals itself, and degrades safely on a read/parse error.
acceptanceCriteria: A helper reloadBody(entry: CronEntry): string is exported from scripts/cron-runtime.ts (exported so tests can call it directly; fire/fireTmux are module-private and LOG_FILE is import-time bound)
On success it returns parseCronFile(fs.readFileSync(entry.filePath, 'utf-8'), path.basename(entry.filePath))?.body

reloadBody owns BOTH log signals (no diff/log logic at call sites): logs BODY_RELOADED when the fresh body differs from cached entry.body; logs BODY_RELOAD_ERR and returns cached entry.body on any read/parse error (missing, unreadable, unparseable, or null parse)
BODY_RELOAD_ERR message includes the cron id/filename and a truncated error string (log() truncates msg to 200 chars)
Helper does NOT mutate entry in place (entry.body stays the boot-time cached value)
Typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: fire() and fireTmux() use reloadBody
userStories.description: As an operator, I want each fire to use the freshly-read body so cron edits deploy at the next fire without a restart.
acceptanceCriteria: fireTmux() computes const body = reloadBody(entry) once and writes body to the prompt file instead of entry.body (replacing line-133 usage); exactly one prompt file written per fire, no extra temp file
fire() computes reloadBody(entry) once and passes it to spawn instead of entry.body (replacing line-157 usage)
Call sites contain NO diff or logging logic — BODY_RELOADED is emitted inside reloadBody (per US-002)
No change to schedule registration, the overlap/SKIPPED_OVERLAP guard, lock semantics, or tmux session naming
Typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: Tests for reload + fallback via appendFileSync spy
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want tests proving an edited body is used and an unreadable file falls back safely.
acceptanceCriteria: cron-runtime.test.ts imports the exported reloadBody and adds a case asserting a body mutated on disk after the CronEntry was built (via loadCrons(tmp) or a hand-built entry with a dir-qualified filePath) is the body returned by reloadBody

A second case asserts an unreadable/removed filePath makes reloadBody return cached entry.body and emit BODY_RELOAD_ERR; the log assertion uses vi.spyOn(fs, 'appendFileSync') to capture the call, NOT a tmp-dir log file (LOG_FILE is import-time bound)
A third assertion (may share a case) confirms BODY_RELOADED is emitted when the on-disk body differs, via the same spy
parseCronFile signature and throw-safety are unchanged, so cron-runtime.property.test.ts needs no edits
Existing tests in cron-runtime.test.ts and cron-runtime.property.test.ts are not modified or removed (new it()/describe() blocks allowed)
Tests pass (vitest run green, including new cases)
Typecheck passes (tsc --noEmit)

userStories.title: Document hot-reload in crons/README.md
userStories.description: As a reader of the cron docs, I want the hot-reload contract documented so the old 'restart the runtime to apply' assumption is retired.
acceptanceCriteria: crons/README.md gains a ## Hot-reload section (<=6 lines of prose)

It states: body edits apply at the next fire without a restart; an unreadable file falls back to the cached body and logs BODY_RELOAD_ERR; a BODY_RELOADED line marks a fire whose body drifted from the boot-time body (recurs each fire until restart); schedule/enabled/timezone changes require a runtime restart — no watcher is implemented
It includes the one-line rollback (drop reloadBody, restore the two entry.body usages; no config flag)
No existing crons/README.md content is removed or reordered (additive section only)
